cards.back: A distributed system is a collection of independent computers that appear to be a single cohesive system to the user, with each computer or node in the system performing a specific function or set of functions.

cards.front: 5.2: Factors affecting the performance of a distributed system
cards.back: Three main factors affecting the performance of a distributed system are: communication overhead, data transfer time, and synchronization overhead.
Two main types of distributed systems are: (1) homogeneous systems where all nodes have the same capabilities, and (2) heterogeneous systems where nodes have different capabilities.
Key characteristics of a distributed system include: decentralized control, autonomy of nodes, and heterogeneity of nodes.
Two main types of distributed architectures are: (1) client-server architecture, and (2) peer-to-peer architecture.
